topTargets.name: KC Concepcion
topTargets.reasoning: At 1.08 the Tier 1 quartet (Love, Tate, Tyson, Lemon) is gone and Sadiq/Mendoza/Price likely off too. Concepcion is Consensus #8 Tier 2 (CLE) and RSP DoT 86.3 (#5 overall, Starter grade) with MFL ADP 13.57 — all three boards converge here, which under our 50/25/25 blend makes him the cleanest WR value at the slot. Roster only has 3 WRs, so the need is real.

topTargets.name: Jonah Coleman
topTargets.reasoning: If Concepcion is sniped, Coleman (Consensus #10 Tier 2, DEN) is the fallback at 1.08 — ADP 20.19 is a slight reach but Consensus Tier 2 RB to Denver is the kind of post-draft landing spot a medium-affinity GM trusts. Backfield is 4-deep but turnover is coming with 6 expiring contracts.

topTargets.name: Emmett Johnson
topTargets.reasoning: Round 2 anchor target across our five 2nd-rounders. Consensus #13 Tier 2 (KC), RSP DoT 84.6 (#8, Rotational Starter, Waldman comps to Harold Fannin). Likely available at 2.07 or 2.08. KC landing spot is a tiebreaker that medium-affinity owners reward heavily.

topTargets.name: Omar Cooper Jr.
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #12 Tier 2 (NYJ paired with Sadiq), RSP DoT 83.9 (#15), MFL ADP 17.20 — the rare board where all three sources rank him within five spots. Realistic 2.02 target if he slides past the early-2nd RB run. Deebo-clone profile fits a thin WR room.

topTargets.name: Eli Stowers
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #21 Tier 3 (PHI), RSP DoT 84.4 (#11, on-cusp Starter), ADP 21.25 — a perfect 2.14/2.18 value. Roster has 3 TEs but no clear young anchor; Stowers in Philly's offense is an asymmetric bet a balanced board respects.

topTargets.name: Mike Washington Jr.
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #11 Tier 2 (LV), RSP DoT 82.5, ADP 24.09. The Consensus/ADP gap (#11 vs ADP 24) is exactly the kind of arbitrage a 10-pick portfolio exploits — likely available at 2.14 or 2.18 well below his Consensus rank.

capPosture: Comfortable at $32.4M of $45M used ($12.6M space), but 10 incoming picks across R1-3 forces aggressive taxi usage and likely 2-3 trade-downs or pick packages — cannot active-roster all 10 rookies even at 50% taxi rates.

wildcard.name: Antonio Williams
wildcard.reasoning: Consensus #24 Tier 4 (WAS) but RSP DoT 83.9 (#18, Jayden Reed comp) — RSP loves him a full tier above Consensus, and ADP 29.06 confirms he's available at 3.07 or 3.08. Medium-affinity GM with Round 3 ammo and a thin WR room takes the RSP-flavored swing.

summary: MWS owns 10 of the first 48 picks and is running a volume-and-value strategy, not a star-hunt. With WR (3) and RB (4) thin and 6 contracts expiring, the brief is to leave the draft with 2-3 immediate-contributor skill players plus 4-5 taxi-stash dart throws. At 1.08 they take the Consensus Tier 2 WR or RB whose ADP confirms (Concepcion ideal, Coleman fallback), then mine the Consensus/ADP arbitrage in rounds 2-3 — Emmett Johnson, Stowers, Washington Jr., Antonio Williams — rather than chasing names. Cap room is fine if discipline holds and most rookies hit taxi at 50% base.
